//scheduleEnabled: Display-only mirror of the workflow cron for the dashboard. Keep in sync with .github/workflows/triage.yml: false = the `schedule:` block is commented out (manual-only), true = the daily cron is active. Does NOT control the cron itself — GitHub reads that from the workflow.
//models: Model per drafting stage (passed to the claude CLI --model). classify picks the category AND writes the clarification reply (the linchpin — one cheap call); mechanical/creative then draft that category's edit with their own model. Blank/omitted = the CLI's account default. Accepts full IDs (claude-opus-4-8, claude-sonnet-5) or aliases (opus, sonnet). If an ID is rejected the comment degrades to clarification — fix it here.

figma.//teamIds: Team IDs (the number in figma.com/files/team/<ID>/...). Auto-discovers every file in the team. 1076153841164353645 = the Mitch Designs (Pro) team — holds all client projects (Apps, Corporate Websites, eCommerce, Landing Pages, Go Group, Resources). Ahmed is a member, so his PAT can walk it.
figma.//fileKeys: Explicit files to watch (the code in figma.com/design/<KEY>/...). Seeded with the El Alsson case-study file.
figma.//extraHandles: Extra names/aliases that count as a mention of you, in addition to your Figma handle from GET /v1/me.
figma.//includeAllUnresolved: If true, include every unresolved comment (not just ones mentioning you). Noisy — leave false. Set false now that team-wide discovery is on (walking ~205 team files with this true would triage every designer's comments).

slack.//enabled: Master switch for Slack output (digest + per-clarification messages). false = dashboard-only: the run still classifies, drafts, and writes jobs/ files, it just doesn't post to Slack. Flip true to resume Slack notifications.
slack.//channelLabel: Cosmetic — shown in logs. The real destination is the SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL secret.

//jobsBaseRawUrl: TODO(config): raw base URL the Figma plugin fetches the day's jobs from, e.g. https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<owner>/<repo>/main/jobs . Repo can be public — it holds no secrets (those live in GitHub Actions secrets).
